tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Boeing 747 is one of the most iconic passenger airplanes ever built and is known for its distinctive hump design.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: The aircraft can carry hundreds of passengers and travel long distances across continents.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Boeing 747 is often called the 'Queen of the Skies'.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Airbus A380 is the world's largest passenger aircraft with a full double-deck design.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It can carry over 800 passengers depending on configuration.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The A380 offers luxury features such as onboard lounges and private suites in some airlines.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The F-22 Raptor is a fifth-generation stealth fighter jet used by the United States Air Force.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It features stealth technology, supercruise capability and advanced avionics.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The F-22 is considered one of the most advanced fighter aircraft in the world.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The F-35 Lightning II is a multi-role stealth fighter used by several countries.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It has advanced sensors, stealth technology and network-centric warfare capabilities.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The F-35 program is one of the most expensive military aircraft programs ever.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The B-2 Spirit is a stealth strategic bomber designed for penetrating dense anti-aircraft defenses.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: The aircraft has a flying wing design that reduces radar visibility.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The B-2 bomber is one of the most expensive aircraft ever built.
